title: Matter, Energy, and Force Practice Test

questions.questionText: What is water in its solid form called?
questions.explanation: Ice is solid water. When water freezes, it becomes ice.

questions.questionText: What do we need to see things?
questions.explanation: Light helps us see. Without light, we cannot see objects.

questions.questionText: Which force pulls things toward the Earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity pulls everything toward the Earth.

questions.questionText: What happens when we heat water?
questions.explanation: When we heat water, it boils and turns into steam.

questions.questionText: Which of these produces sound?
questions.explanation: A ringing bell vibrates and produces sound.

questions.questionText: What do we call a push or a pull?
questions.explanation: A push or a pull is called a force.

questions.questionText: How many states of matter are there?
questions.explanation: Matter has three states: solid, liquid, and gas.

questions.questionText: Which object gives us light at night?
questions.explanation: The Moon reflects sunlight and gives us light at night.

questions.questionText: What slows down moving objects?
questions.explanation: Friction between surfaces slows down moving objects.

questions.questionText: Which is a liquid?
questions.explanation: Milk is a liquid. It flows and takes the shape of its container.

questions.questionText: How does sound travel to our ears?
questions.explanation: Sound travels through air (and other materials) to reach our ears.

questions.questionText: What do we need to make a shadow?
questions.explanation: A shadow forms when light is blocked by an object.

questions.questionText: Which is a gas?
questions.explanation: Air is a mixture of gases. We cannot see it but we can feel it.

questions.questionText: What happens when we throw a ball up?
questions.explanation: Gravity pulls the ball back down toward the Earth.

questions.questionText: Which gives us heat and light?
questions.explanation: The Sun gives us heat and light during the day.

questions.questionText: What do we use to measure how hot or cold something is?
questions.explanation: A thermometer measures temperature.

questions.questionText: Which object makes a loud sound?
questions.explanation: A drum makes a loud sound when we hit it.

questions.questionText: What do magnets attract?
questions.explanation: Magnets attract objects made of iron and some other metals.

questions.questionText: When does ice melt?
questions.explanation: Ice melts when we heat it. It turns into liquid water.

questions.questionText: Can sound travel through empty space?
questions.explanation: Sound needs a medium like air to travel. In space there is no air.

questions.questionText: Which is a solid?
questions.explanation: A table is a solid. It has a fixed shape and size.

questions.questionText: What do we need to move a heavy box?
questions.explanation: We need to push or pull (apply force) to move a heavy box.

questions.questionText: Why do we see a rainbow after rain?
options: Because sunlight splits in water drops
questions.explanation: Sunlight splits into colors when it passes through water drops.

questions.questionText: What is the main source of energy on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the main source of light and heat energy on Earth.

questions.questionText: Which has a fixed shape?
questions.explanation: A book is a solid and has a fixed shape.
